title: Conic Sections
subject: Mathematics
content:
# Conic Sections

## Quick Synopsis

Conics are locus curves defined by focus-directrix or standard equations. JEE focuses on standard forms, tangents, normals, chords, and parametric points.

## Must Know

- Parabola has eccentricity 1.
- Ellipse has eccentricity less than 1; hyperbola has eccentricity greater than 1.
- Standard orientation matters for formula selection.
- Parametric forms simplify tangents and normals.
- Auxiliary circle helps ellipse geometry.
- Rectangular hyperbola xy = c² has special tangent/normal properties.

## Formulas / Rules

- Parabola y² = 4ax; focus (a,0), directrix x = -a.
- Ellipse x²/a² + y²/b² = 1; b² = a²(1-e²).
- Hyperbola x²/a² - y²/b² = 1; b² = a²(e²-1).
- Tangent to parabola y²=4ax at t: ty = x + at².
- Tangent to ellipse at (x1,y1): xx1/a² + yy1/b² = 1.
- Tangent to hyperbola at (x1,y1): xx1/a² - yy1/b² = 1.

## JEE Focus

- Memorize standard parametric points for parabola, ellipse, hyperbola.
- Use chord with given midpoint via T = S1 method.
- For pair of tangents from point, use combined equation SS1 = T².
- Compare eccentricity to identify conic quickly.

## Common Traps

- Major axis changes formula if b > a.
- Asymptotes are approached but not touched at infinity.
- Normal formulas differ from tangent formulas.
- Do not mix horizontal and vertical parabola formulas.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you identify conic from equation?
- Can you write tangent/normal?
- Can you use parametric coordinates?
- Can you solve chord of contact and midpoint chord questions?
